name: Anthurium forgetii 'Variegated'
origin: Species native to Colombia; variegated collector form in cultivation
aboutText: Anthurium forgetii 'Variegated' is a stunning, compact understory aroid native to Colombia. Highly prized in collector circles, it is famous for its distinctive, nearly round, sinusless leaves that feature a plush velvety texture and contrasting pale venation. The rare variegated form is adorned with breathtaking cream-to-yellow marbling and sectoral variegation splashed across its deep green leaf blades. In cultivation, it performs best in warm, highly humid conditions with a very airy, well-draining substrate and bright indirect light.

morphology.variegation: Cream to yellow marbling and sectoral splashes

fieldNotes.title: The Circular Jewel of Colombia
fieldNotes.author: Ariod Aaron
fieldNotes.content: Anthurium forgetii is instantly recognizable because it completely lacks the upper sinus (the lobes at the top of the leaf are fused, making it almost circular). The green velvet texture is outstanding, but the variegated cultivar adds a new dimension with splashes of yellow and cream. A 2019 genetic study on Colombian understory aroids highlighted the species' unique leaf geometry as a highly specialized design to reduce water accumulation on the leaf blade, preventing fungal growth. In cultivation, it is slow and demands extremely high humidity.
